symptoms.symptom_name: Solitary herald patch preceding the eruption
symptoms.onset: approximately one week before the generalised eruption
symptoms.character: single larger oval scaly erythematous plaque

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A herald patch preceding the widespread eruption by 1-2 weeks is characteristic of pityriasis rosea.
Tinea expands slowly as annular plaques rather than seeding a sudden diffuse secondary eruption from one patch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread symmetrical oval scaly maculopapular truncal eruption
symptoms.character: multiple small salmon-coloured oval scaly macules and papules on trunk and proximal limbs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical oval scaly truncal macules are the typical secondary eruption of pityriasis rosea.
A sudden scaly truncal eruption in a young adult overlaps with guttate psoriasis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Collarette scaling with lesions along cleavage lines (Christmas-tree distribution)
symptoms.character: inward-facing collarette of fine scale; lesions aligned to Langer's lines producing a Christmas-tree pattern on the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inward collarette scaling plus cleavage-line (Christmas-tree) distribution is essentially diagnostic of pityriasis rosea — but requires in-person examination and cannot be confirmed over chat.
Psoriasis shows whole-lesion silvery micaceous scale rather than an inward collarette.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding mild viral prodrome (fatigue, sore throat) ~2 weeks earlier

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A preceding viral prodrome is consistent with the HHV-6/7 reactivation associated with pityriasis rosea.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sparing of the palms and soles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secondary syphilis characteristically involves the palms and soles; their sparing is a strong argument against it.
Pityriasis rosea characteristically spares the palms, soles and face.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of systemic toxicity (no fever, arthralgia, mucosal ulceration)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional symptoms and mucosal lesions accompany secondary syphilis; their absence argues against it.
Systemic features and mucosal involvement would raise a severe cutaneous drug reaction; their absence is reassuring.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new medication in the preceding weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A drug eruption requires a temporally related new medication; its absence effectively excludes a drug cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild to moderate pruritus aggravated by heat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild pruritus (heat-aggravated) is common in pityriasis rosea but non-specific across scaly eruptions.
Mild lymphadenopathy can accompany the post-viral picture but is non-specific; requires in-person examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: KOH microscopy of skin scraping (would be negative)

investigation_details.expected_result: No fungal hyphae seen — does not support tinea; only helpful if the diagnosis is in doubt or the herald patch were isolated.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: None in a classic presentation — the widespread eruption is clinically diagnostic and KOH is not required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative KOH argues against tinea, but is reserved for diagnostic doubt rather than routine use here.

investigation_details.expected_result: Non-reactive — excludes secondary syphilis; reserved for atypical presentations or where sexual-risk factors are disclosed.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: In a classic palm/sole-sparing presentation without risk factors, serology is not mandatory; it becomes appropriate if the picture is atypical.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-reactive serology definitively excludes secondary syphilis where clinical doubt remains.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: No single feature is fully diagnostic, but a herald patch followed by a symmetrical oval scaly truncal eruption with inward collarette scaling along cleavage lines together constitute the pathognomonic pityriasis rosea constellation.

Palm/sole sparing + absence of systemic toxicity + no new medication together exclude the dangerous mimics (secondary syphilis, drug eruption/SJS), converting a frightening rash into a confident benign diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required in a classic palm/sole-sparing presentation; appropriate only if the picture is atypical or sexual-risk factors are disclosed. Ordering it reflexively is acceptable but not mandatory.
The widespread classic eruption is clinically diagnostic; KOH is only useful if the diagnosis is in doubt or the herald patch were isolated. Routine ordering is over-investigation.
Reserved for atypical or persistent rashes (>12 weeks, to exclude parapsoriasis variants) — not indicated in a classic self-limiting presentation.
